content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, construct an array AAA of length NNN such that:


1‚Č§Ai‚Č§10181 \leq A_i \leq 10^{18}1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§1018 for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N);
There exists a subsequence of length **greater** than 111 such that the gcd of all elements of the subsequence is iii, for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.

More formally, for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N), there exists a subsequence SSS of array AAA such that the length of the subsequence is k(2‚Č§k‚Č§N)k (2 \leq k \leq N)k(2‚Č§k‚Č§N) and gcd(S1,S2,‚Ä¶,Sk)=igcd(S_1,S_2,\ldots,S_k)=igcd(S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Sk‚Äč)=i.
It can be proven that it is always possible to construct such AAA under given constraints. If there exist multiple such arrays, print any.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing NNN space-separated integers, denoting the elements of the array AAA. The ithi^{th}ith of these NNN integers is the ithi^{th}ith element of the array AAA.


If there exist multiple such arrays, print any.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1035 \cdot 10^35‚čÖ103.
